skill.name: upset-plot-converter
skill.description: Convert complex Venn diagrams with more than 4 sets to clearer upset plot visualizations using Python and matplotlib.
skill.summary: Converts complex set intersection data from 4+ sets into readable upset plot visualizations.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 45 static findings are false positives upon manual evaluation. The 27 external command detections are shell commands documented in markdown code blocks for legitimate skill execution (python scripts/main.py, pip install). Certificate/key file detections matched Python dictionary .keys() method calls. Weak cryptographic algorithm detections matched sorting operations and YAML frontmatter text. Path traversal detections matched security checklist items warning against traversal. Hardcoded URL detections matched a source attribution link. No evidence of credential access, data exfiltration, obfuscation, or malicious intent was found. The skill executes a Python script locally and writes PNG output files, which are inherent to its function as a data visualization tool.

low_findings.title: Shell commands in documentation (false positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged 27 instances of shell backtick execution in SKILL.md. All instances are bash commands inside markdown code blocks documenting legitimate skill usage: python scripts/main.py, python -m py_compile, pip install. These are not malicious execution payloads.

low_findings.title: Certificate/key file detection (false positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged .keys() at scripts/main.py:17 and :193 as certificate/key files. This is Python dictionary .keys() method used to retrieve set names, not cryptographic key material.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptographic algorithm detection (false positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged lines in main.py and SKILL.md as weak cryptography. Line 43 of main.py is a sort operation on intersection sizes. Lines 3 and 23 of SKILL.md are YAML frontmatter and skill description text. No cryptographic operations exist in the skill.

low_findings.title: System reconnaissance detection (false positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged matplotlib plotting code in main.py, documentation text in SKILL.md, and evaluation metadata in audit JSON as system reconnaissance. Line 122 of main.py draws grid lines on a matplotlib plot. SKILL.md lines are documentation text. Audit JSON lines are evaluation assertions.

low_findings.title: Path traversal sequence in checklist (false positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged ../ at SKILL.md:153 and :156 as path traversal. These lines are in a security checklist that explicitly warns against path traversal (No unauthorized file system access (../)). This is documentation of a security boundary, not an attack.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL in source citation (false positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged a URL at SKILL.md:7 as a network indicator. This is a source attribution link (https://github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills) in the markdown header, not a programmatic network call.

low_findings.title: Obfuscation heuristic: dangerous combination (false positive)
low_findings.description: Static analyzer flagged a dangerous combination of code execution + network + credential access based on aggregated findings. Since all component findings are false positives, the combination heuristic is also a false positive. The skill has no network access, no credential access, and limited local script execution.

content.user_title: Convert Venn Diagrams to Upset Plots
content.value_statement: Venn diagrams become unreadable when comparing more than 4 sets. This skill automatically converts complex set intersection data into clear, publication-ready upset plot visualizations.
actual_capabilities: Convert dictionary of sets to an upset plot PNG file
Create upset plots from list-based input data with named sets
Filter intersections by minimum subset size and maximum intersection count
Print detailed set intersection statistics with element counts
Generate publication-ready PNG output at 150 DPI resolution
Support for any number of sets beyond Venn diagram limits
limitations: Produces only static PNG output files, not interactive visualizations
Cannot animate or update plots dynamically
Limited to preset styling with basic matplotlib defaults
Large numbers of sets (over 10) produce cluttered visualizations

use_cases.title: Analyze Gene Set Overlaps
use_cases.description: A biomedical researcher compares 5 gene expression datasets to identify common and unique gene signatures across experimental conditions.

use_cases.title: Visualize Customer Segment Intersections
use_cases.description: A data scientist analyzes overlapping customer behavior segments to identify high-value intersection groups for targeted marketing.

use_cases.title: Compare Multiple Experimental Conditions
use_cases.description: An academic researcher visualizes how experimental outcomes overlap across 6 different treatment groups in a controlled study.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Upset Plot from Named Sets
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an upset plot from these 5 sets of experimental results. Set A contains {1,2,3,4,5}, Set B contains {4,5,6,7,8}, Set C contains {3,5,7,9,10}, Set D contains {2,4,6,8,10}, Set E contains {1,3,5,7,9}. Save the output as upset_plot.png.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has named sets as Python dictionaries

prompt_templates.title: Upset Plot from Element Lists
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an upset plot from these gene lists. Gene set names are: Upregulated, Downregulated, Methylated, Mutated. The lists are: ['BRCA1', 'TP53', 'EGFR'], ['TP53', 'KRAS', 'MYC'], ['BRCA1', 'MYC', 'PTEN'], ['EGFR', 'KRAS', 'PTEN']. Save the output as gene_upset.png with title Gene Analysis.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has element lists without explicit set relationships

prompt_templates.title: Filtered Upset Plot with Custom Parameters
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert these 6 sets to an upset plot. Only show intersections with at least 3 elements and limit to the top 15 intersections. Set names: Treatment_A through Treatment_F with values: A={1,2,3,4,5,6}, B={4,5,6,7,8,9}, C={3,5,7,9,10,11}, D={2,4,6,8,10,12}, E={1,3,5,7,9,11}, F={2,5,8,11,14,17}. Save as filtered_upset.png.
prompt_templates.scenario: User needs to reduce visual complexity from many possible intersections

prompt_templates.title: Programmatic Usage with Statistics
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these 7 sets and create an upset plot. First print intersection statistics, then generate the plot. Sets are: Response_A through Response_G with values: A={x1,x2,x3,x4}, B={x3,x4,x5,x6}, C={x2,x4,x6,x7}, D={x1,x5,x7,x8}, E={x3,x6,x8,x9}, F={x4,x7,x9,x10}, G={x1,x8,x10,x11}. Save as full_analysis_upset.png with title 7-Set Response Analysis.
prompt_templates.scenario: User wants both numerical summary and visual output

output_examples.input: 5 sets of patient response data: Drug_A includes patients P1-P5, Drug_B includes P4-P8, Drug_C includes P3,P5,P7,P9,P10, Drug_D includes P2,P4,P6,P8,P10, Drug_E includes P1,P3,P5,P7,P9.
output_examples.output: An upset plot PNG with bar chart showing intersection sizes. The 5 rows show which drug combinations share patients. Largest bar (4 patients) at Drug_A + Drug_E intersection. Dot matrix below bars shows set membership patterns for each intersection.

output_examples.input: 3 gene lists from RNA-seq analysis: Differentially expressed genes (50 genes), Methylated genes (30 genes), Copy-number altered genes (20 genes).
output_examples.output: Upset plot with 3 rows showing 7 possible intersections. Bar chart shows the 15 genes shared between differentially expressed and methylated sets as the largest intersection. Each bar labeled with exact count. Warning message noted that 3 sets work well as a standard Venn diagram.

best_practices: Always specify an output path so the generated plot is saved to a known location.
Use min_subset_size to filter out small, potentially noisy intersections from the visualization.
Set max_intersections to a reasonable value (20-30) for readability when working with many sets.
anti_patterns: Using this skill for 3 or fewer sets when a Venn diagram would provide a more intuitive visualization.
Including duplicate element names across sets which may produce misleading intersection counts.
Expecting real-time interactive output since the skill produces only static PNG files.

faq.question: What is an upset plot?
faq.answer: An upset plot is a visualization that shows set intersections using a bar chart with a dot matrix below. It is easier to read than Venn diagrams when comparing more than 4 sets.

faq.question: How many sets can this skill handle?
faq.answer: The skill works best with 4 to 10 sets. Sets beyond 10 may produce cluttered visualizations. The default maximum number of intersections shown is 30.

faq.question: What output format does the skill produce?
faq.answer: The skill produces a static PNG image file at 150 DPI resolution. The output path is specified by the user when calling the function.

faq.question: How are set intersections calculated?
faq.answer: The skill computes all possible combinations of set intersections. Each element is assigned to the combination of sets it belongs to. Results are sorted by intersection size from largest to smallest.

faq.question: Can I filter which intersections appear in the plot?
faq.answer: Yes. Use min_subset_size to hide intersections smaller than a threshold and max_intersections to limit how many intersections are displayed.

faq.question: What happens if I have fewer than 4 sets?
faq.answer: The skill still generates an upset plot but displays a warning that a Venn diagram may be more appropriate for 3 or fewer sets.
